summary: Create explicit Codex/Claude collaboration protocol, machine-readable sync state and event feed.

Deep-read construction, CoIn, HQ deploy/repair, structure sale and server creation flows; publish a source-backed atlas.
Added Construction-And-CoIn-Systems-Atlas.md and updated navigation, agent context, feature status and worklog.

Turn DR-1 into a behavior-preserving PVF dispatch validation playbook + BattlEye filter design; verify against Chernarus source before writing.
Deep-read factory purchase, buy menu, unit spawn, server AI buying and authority boundaries; publish a source-backed atlas.

Dispatch never needs Call Compile: SRVFNC/CLTFNC targets are missionNamespace globals (Init_PublicVariables.sqf:44,49), so 'Spawn (Call Compile _script)' can become 'Spawn (missionNamespace getVariable [_script,{}])' — closes arbitrary code execution, behavior-identical for legit traffic. Verified no registered PVF handler compiles its parameters (no second-order injection on the PVF path).
Added Progress-Dashboard.md and agent-status.json so Steff, Codex, Claude and future agents can track active lanes and event feeds from one place.

Residual (separate from RCE): PVF model still trusts client-sent commands+params; legitimate-command forgery (e.g. RequestChangeScore sets score from payload) remains until per-handler sender/param validation is added. Scoped out of the dispatch fix to avoid over-claiming.

Added Round-3 PVF hardening implementation playbook to Deep-Review-Findings.md (Fix 1 getVariable dispatch, Fix 2 allow-list, Fix 3 BattlEye filter, residual-risk scope). Handoff: code owners apply in Chernarus source + run LoadoutManager; BattlEyeFilter is deployed with the server, not via LoadoutManager.
Verify construction PVF authority boundary (Codex handoff after construction-coin-atlas); concretize the DR-1 command-forgery residual for RequestStructure/RequestDefense/RequestMHQRepair.

DR-6: construction request handlers do no sender/authority/resource validation. RequestStructure/RequestDefense take _side from the client payload; RequestMHQRepair passes the raw payload to MHQRepair. No commander/funds/dead-HQ checks (client-only). Forge free factories/defenses/HQs for any side. Root cause: payloads omit the player and Arma2 PVEH has no sender identity, so authority must be reconstructed server-side.

Added DR-6 + validation playbook (add player to payload; server checks side+commander+funds; base-area avail server-side) to Deep-Review-Findings.md Round 4. Deepens Construction-And-CoIn-Systems-Atlas Authority Boundary with per-handler forgery proof. Handoff: code owners; pair with DR-1 (DR-1 stops code execution, DR-6 stops forged legit commands).

Self-authored long-term goal + Codebase-Coverage-Ledger.md: subsystem x dimension (Map/Auth/PV/Perf/JIP-HC/Drift) matrix driving systematic full-codebase comprehension. Handoff to Codex: add page to navigation (Codex-owned).

Next ledger lane: deep-review the AntiStack external DB extension trust path (Server/Module/AntiStack/callDatabase*.sqf + A2WaspDatabase DLL) — numeric procedure codes, player-list/skill persistence, live-server trust boundary.

DR-7 (High): all 7 callDatabase*.sqf do 'call compile' on the A2WaspDatabase callExtension return — server executes the external DLL's stdout as SQF. DLL is not in the repo; module defaults ON (ANTISTACK_ENABLED=1, Init_CommonConstants.sqf:171). Empty/garbage return -> call compile '' -> nil select 0 throws. Arma2 OA has no parseSimpleArray, so fix is defensive shape-validation, not a parser swap.

DR-8 blocking poll on join/skill path (RETRIEVE 120x0.1s~12s; REQUEST_SIDE_SKILL 9x3s=27s). DR-9 SEND_PLAYERLIST packs whole roster into one callExtension call vs A2 OA length limits -> truncation risk. DR-10 defaults-on against an absent external DLL -> error spam unless param set 0.

Added Deep-Review-Findings Round 5 (DR-7..DR-10) with defensive-validation + circuit-breaker + chunking + DLL-detection playbooks; advanced ledger Integrations Auth/PV/Perf/JIP cells to partial. Handoff: code owners harden the 7 handlers; Codex document the A2WaspDatabase external dependency + call-compile contract on External-Integrations (its lane).

Added Factory-And-Purchase-Systems-Atlas.md and updated navigation/context/risk status. Key finding: player buy-unit path is client-local with no RequestBuyUnit PVF; Server_BuyUnit/AIBuyUnit appears latent unless a dynamic caller is later proven.

Ledger: victory/endgame was fully empty + high-traffic. Review server_victory_threeway.sqf, score persistence and player-list flush.

DR-11 (Med-High): victory condition merges a lose-test (HQ dead + no factories) and a win-test (holds all towns) into one if and handles both identically. WFBE_CO_FNC_LogGameEnd (arg=winner) is called with the OPPOSITE of _x, so the persisted *_WIN_CHERNARUS tally is INVERTED for all-towns victories. WF_Winner is a dead write (no reader). && binds before || and !WFBE_GameOver guards only the towns branch + no forEach break -> endgame can double-fire.

DR-12: WFBE_C_VICTORY_THREEWAY defaults 0; detection gated if(_victory==0); this is the ONLY gameOver/failMission setter in Server/ -> selecting threeway mode = matches never auto-end (broken/abandoned feature). DR-13: two LogGameEnd impls; Server/PVFunctions/LogGameEnd.sqf is buggy (getVariable result used as setVariable key; bare-global WEST_WIN_CHERNARUS) — delete to avoid mis-wiring.

Added Deep-Review-Findings Round 6 (DR-11..DR-13); advanced ledger Victory/endgame row. Handoff: code owners fix winner-inversion (split branches/guard/break), decide threeway, delete buggy LogGameEnd dup. Follow-up: WFBE_CL_FNC_EndGame payload semantics.
Ledger unblocked by Codex factory-purchase-atlas. Verify player purchase authority + adversarially check Cicero's Server_AssignNewCommander call-shape candidate.

DR-14: player purchasing has NO server authority — GUI_Menu_BuyUnits spawns BuildUnit (client createVehicle/createUnit) and deducts client-side; no RequestBuyUnit PVF. Architectural WFBE locality ceiling; only a BattlEye scripts.txt filter (not PV filter) can constrain client createVehicle. This bounds what DR-1/DR-6 hardening can achieve for the player economy.

DR-15 CONFIRMED (was Cicero candidate): Server_AssignNewCommander.sqf:3 '_side = _this' but sole caller RequestNewCommander.sqf:13 passes [_side,_commander]; so _side=the array, GetSideLogic fails, AI-commander-stop block mis-fires. Fix: _side = _this select 0. Also redundant new-commander-assigned broadcast (RequestNewCommander + AssignNewCommander).

Ledger: UI/HUD authority cell. Review economy-menu structure-sale authority (DR-6 sibling) + adversarially verify Curie's duplicate-IDD-23000 candidate.

DR-16: GUI_Menu_Economy.sqf:104-152 structure sale is fully client-authoritative — client-side commander check, client refund (ChangeSideSupply/ChangePlayerFunds), client setDammage 1 to destroy; no server PVF. Completes the economy picture: build (DR-6), buy (DR-14), sell (DR-16) all client-authoritative. Fix: align casing or getVariable default 0. DR-20 (High): HQ Killed EH is registered on EVERY owning-side client (set-hq-killed-eh broadcast + Init_Client.sqf:500-503 JIP path), but Server_OnHQKilled.sqf has no idempotency guard -> on mobile-HQ death the server runs OnHQKilled N times (N = owning-side clients): ~2N x killer score award + N messages. JIP detection itself is correct (Init_Client guard !_isDeployed). Fix: per-HQ processed flag in OnHQKilled (detect redundantly, act once).

DR-21 + self-correction: round-1 'HC disconnect orphans units' is WRONG — Arma2 OA migrates a disconnecting machine's local units to the server (no loss). Real effects: server load spike (offloaded AI lands on server) + NO re-delegation/failover (disconnect handler doesn't reassign; delegation only evaluated at boot). Suggest setGroupOwner-based rebalancing on HC connect/disconnect (mission currently never uses setGroupOwner).

Added Deep-Review-Findings Round 11 (DR-21 + corrected round-1 orphan hypothesis). Advanced ledger AI/Headless JIP/HC. Handoff: HC delegation has no failover today; add rebalancing if HC stability matters.
Confirm Faraday's negative side-supply delta candidate + my round-1 inverted-guard note at Common/Server_ChangeSideSupply.sqf.

DR-22 (High, economy exploit): Server_ChangeSideSupply.sqf clamp 'if (_change<0) then {_change=_currentSupply-_amount}' is a broken floor — _amount is signed, deductions negative, so overspending (e.g. spend 300 from 100) yields 100-(-300)=400, a windfall. Fix: _change=0. Same block in Common_ChangeSideSupply is dead (PV sends _amount; server recomputes). Confirms Faraday candidate + sharpens round-1 note. No resistance-side handler (round-1, still open).

Forge free upgrades for any side; client-controlled indices -> out-of-range error. Confirms Faraday candidate. GLOBALGAMESTATS extension: SAFE today (RvExtension _output never written; SQF callExtension return discarded at GlobalGameStats.sqf:22 -> no call compile of output, contrast DR-7; reflection gated by Enum.TryParse). BUT: (2) dormant deser-RCE landmine - commented load path SerializationManager.cs:115-120 uses TypeNameHandling.Auto + DeserializeObject (Newtonsoft $type gadget) - Critical if re-enabled; (3) write-only/abandoned-refactor stub - no cross-restart persistence, load path commented + references different type graph (Database vs live GameData), stale new string[2] + Todo comments; (4) async void SerializeDB calls async-void CheckIfFile... unawaited then File.Replace -> first-run FileNotFound race; async-void exceptions can crash host; (5) minor: SQF abs(playerCount-1) HC heuristic misreports.

Source-verify the repo BattlEye footprint to confirm whether option (b) rely-on-BattlEye in the DR-1/6/14/16/22/23/27/28 owner-decisions is actually shipped.

BattlEye mitigation NOT shipped. Only BE filter in repo is BattlEyeFilter/publicvariable.txt = 22 bytes, single rule 5 "kickAFK" (the AFK-kick feature plumbing, not a security control). No default-deny catch-all -> no restriction on RequestSpecial(ICBM DR-27)/RequestStructure(DR-6)/RequestUpgrade(DR-23)/HandlePVF(DR-1). scripts.txt ABSENT (+createvehicle/remoteexec/setvariable/setpos/mpeventhandler) -> nothing blunts the DR-1 call compile RCE. 716KB README.docx not parsed (binary/untrusted). Implication: option (b) rely-on-BattlEye in 8 prior findings is illusory as-shipped; remediation collapses to (a) server-side authority. Caveat: BE filters normally live in server BEpath outside the PBO, so production posture is an owner question.

Review in-repo DiscordBot (.NET) as the consumer of GLOBALGAMESTATS database.json; check deserialization posture, secret hygiene, inbound command trust.

DiscordBot GameData.LoadFromFile() (GameData.cs:49-56) deserializes database.json with TypeNameHandling.All - the Newtonsoft gadget RCE sink, worse than the .Auto I flagged dormant in DR-29. Called every 60s by GameStatusUpdater timer (+startup ProgramRuntime.cs:15 +command CommandHandler.cs:211), no interaction. Capability gratuitous: GameData is a flat string[] DTO, writer uses TypeNameHandling.None. Not remote as-configured (file locally written by trusted extension) but any write-primitive to C:/a2waspwarfare/Data/database.json = RCE in the token-holding bot process. Fix: .All -> .None + delete dead .Auto method (GameDataDeSerialization.cs:32, no callers). Secondary: secret hygiene GOOD (token.txt+preferences.json gitignored, sample has no token - resolves reports Discord-hygiene item); commands auth-gated (IsUserAuthorized); 3-way exportedArgs shape drift (ext[2]/bot[4]/SQF sends 5).

DR-29 deserialization concern closed end-to-end (dormant in writer, live in reader, one-token fix). Handoff: Codex document Discord data path in External-Integrations (one-way pull; hygiene OK; commands gated); flag TypeNameHandling.All->None as the actionable code-owner item; cross-link DR-29/DR-31.

Fill Factory/purchase Perf + JIP-HC cells: review Client_BuildUnit production loop + GUI_Menu_BuyUnits queue gate + WFBE_C_QUEUE counters.

Factory production runs on buyer client. Research autonomy: walk the direct publicVariableServer channels (PV index) for the forgery class DR-41 opened; first target wfbe_supply_temp_<side>.

wfbe_supply_temp_<side> is a forgeable DIRECT PV. Common_ChangeSideSupply.sqf:28-30 publicVariableServer [_side,_amount,_reason]; Server_ChangeSideSupply.sqf (PVEH :1 west/:25 east) takes _amount straight from payload, _change=_currentSupply+_amount, sets wfbe_supply_<side> (capped at max) - NO sender auth, NO _side-vs-sender check, NO re-derivation. Forged wfbe_supply_temp_west=[west,999999,x] = arbitrary side-supply inflation. Supply gates attack-wave/funds/production. Author knew (the _reason fallback says 'might indicate a malicious supply update request') but logged instead of validating. 2nd confirmed direct-PV forgery after DR-41 -> direct-channel surface is a class. Distinct from DR-22 (broken floor, same file). Extends economy thesis: the supply LEDGER itself is client-writable.

PR8 June-finalize batch + author-scrub/force-push heads-up for Codex. (1) NEW gameplay feature on release (e338c9be, EXPLICITLY approved by Steff): Common_JetAADamage.sqf HandleDamage on player jets (isKindOf Plane). First ground-SPAAG hit (2S6M_Tunguska or M6_EP1) drains fuel to 0 + slight non-lethal damage so the pilot can dead-stick land and escape; a second separate hit always explodes; service-point repair restores fuel to 100 and re-arms; a crash still pays the shooter via the existing 60s delayed last-hit attribution (RequestOnUnitKilled). Constant WFBE_C_JET_AA_SURVIVE (default 1) disables. Attached client- and server-side for locality parity with the existing AA Fired handlers; mirrored to Takistan. (2) Town patrols now ON by default on release (WFBE_C_TOWNS_PATROLS=6; DR-57 fix retained; lobby 0 disables). (3) SCRUB/FORCE-PUSH HEADS-UP: per Steff all PR commit authorship was rewritten to rayswaynl (filter-branch) and Claude refs removed from commit messages and from the PR8/PR21 bodies; release/2026-06-feature-bundle, tools/reusable-pr-test-harness and dev/july-2026-update were FORCE-PUSHED. Old SHAs in earlier events (eg release 37914838) are STALE - Codex must fetch and hard-reset/rebase to the new heads. Current heads: release e338c9be (PR8), harness aa2ca3da (PR20), july b502cb4c (PR21). (4) Upstream note (Miksuu/a2waspwarfare): the recent town-defense outage was an A3-only String-find-String in Server_CleanupTownDefenseGroupPool.sqf - introduced f7bbd9519 (Marty865), patched a9b7a2e42, then rolled back by 307c6b5f restore-legacy (merge 12a2ce6f).
